questions.text: Which is the collective noun for a group of wolves?
questions.explanation: A group of wolves is called a pack.

questions.text: The abstract noun of 'happy' is ____.
questions.explanation: The abstract noun of happy is happiness.

questions.text: 'Bravery' is an abstract noun.
questions.explanation: Bravery is a quality, so abstract. True.

questions.text: Choose the correct verb: 'Neither of the boys ____ here.'
questions.explanation: 'Neither' is singular, so we use 'is'.

questions.text: The past participle of 'speak' is ____.
questions.explanation: speak → spoke → spoken (past participle).

questions.text: In 'The cake was eaten', the verb is in passive voice.
questions.explanation: 'was eaten' is passive voice. True.

questions.text: Choose the superlative: 'good, better, ____'.
questions.explanation: good → better → best (irregular).

questions.text: Comparative of 'bad' is ____.
questions.explanation: bad → worse → worst.

questions.text: The comparative of 'beautiful' is 'beautifuller'.
questions.explanation: It is 'more beautiful', not 'beautifuller'. False.

questions.text: Which is an adverb of frequency?
questions.explanation: 'Always' tells how often, so it is an adverb of frequency.

questions.text: Adverb of 'happy' is ____.
questions.explanation: happy → happily.

questions.text: 'Everywhere' is an adverb of place.
questions.explanation: 'Everywhere' tells where, so adverb of place. True.

questions.text: Identify the tense: 'They had finished their work.'
questions.explanation: 'had finished' is past perfect tense.

questions.text: By next year, she ____ completed her course. (will have)
questions.explanation: Future perfect uses 'will have'.

questions.text: 'I was playing' is past continuous tense.
questions.explanation: 'was playing' is past continuous. True.

questions.text: Choose the synonym of 'brave'.
questions.explanation: 'Courageous' means the same as brave.

questions.text: Antonym of 'expand' is ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of expand is shrink.

questions.text: 'Enormous' and 'tiny' are antonyms.
questions.explanation: Enormous (huge) and tiny are opposites. True.

questions.text: Which sentence is punctuated correctly?
questions.explanation: A question starts with a capital and ends with '?'.

questions.text: We use a ____ to separate items in a list.
questions.explanation: Commas separate items in a list.

questions.text: An apostrophe (') can show possession, like 'Ram's book'.
questions.explanation: Apostrophe shows possession. True.

questions.text: Fill: 'He is good ____ mathematics.'
questions.explanation: We say 'good at' a subject/skill.

questions.text: The plane flew ____ the clouds.
questions.explanation: The plane flew above the clouds.

questions.text: We do not use 'the' before the names of most countries like 'India'.
questions.explanation: Most country names don't take 'the' (e.g., India). True.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the idiom 'a piece of cake'?
questions.explanation: 'A piece of cake' means something very easy.

questions.text: The idiom 'break the ice' means to ____.
questions.explanation: 'Break the ice' means to start a conversation.

questions.text: The plural of 'leaf' is 'leaves'.
questions.explanation: leaf → leaves. True.

questions.text: Which conjunction joins two opposite ideas?
questions.explanation: 'But' joins contrasting (opposite) ideas.

questions.text: Direct: He said, 'I am tired.' → Indirect: He said that he ____ tired.
questions.explanation: In reported speech, present 'am' changes to past 'was'.

questions.text: A sentence must have at least a subject and a verb.
questions.explanation: A complete sentence needs a subject and a verb. True.
